changeSet.author: travel-insurance
changeSet.comment:
Fix: recalculate current_usage_count from promo_code_usage table.
Replaces the incorrect approach from 011-01 (reset all counters to 0) with correct recalculation based on actual usage history.
Formula: current_usage_count = COUNT(*) in promo_code_usage per promo_code_id
For promo codes that have never been used (no records in promo_code_usage), the counter is correctly set to 0.

changeSet.author: travel-insurance
changeSet.comment:
Verification: ensure current_usage_count is consistent with promo_code_usage.
Raises an exception if any promo code has a counter that differs from the actual count in promo_code_usage — protecting against data inconsistencies introduced by future migrations or direct DB edits.
This check runs immediately after recalculation (021-01) as a guard.

changeSet.author: travel-insurance
changeSet.comment: Update column comment to document correct semantics and migration history.
changeSet.sql:
COMMENT ON COLUMN promo_codes.current_usage_count IS 'Number of times this promo code has been successfully applied.
SOURCE OF TRUTH: promo_code_usage table (COUNT per promo_code_id).
IMPORTANT — Migration history: 011-01 (INCORRECT): Reset all counters to 0 unconditionally. This was a bug — it erased real usage data for codes that had already been applied. 021-01 (FIX): Recalculated from promo_code_usage.
MAINTENANCE: This counter is incremented transactionally in PromoCodeService via pessimistic locking (SELECT FOR UPDATE) to prevent race conditions. Never update this column directly; always go through the service layer.
CONSISTENCY CHECK: current_usage_count should always equal: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM promo_code_usage WHERE promo_code_id = id';
